page.content: The data presented in the following table offers a comprehensive overview of the associations between various CpG clusters and their corresponding metrics in relation to outbreak investigations. Specifically, the CpG cluster identified as 1–2 demonstrates a statistically significant β value of 0.004, indicating a notable correlation with the outcomes of report-driven outbreak investigations. The sample size, denoted by the column 'n,' includes a robust number of participants, which enhances the reliability of the findings. Furthermore, the p value of 0.98 suggests that while the correlation is present, the statistical significance may require further investigation to establish definitive conclusions. The interplay between the S-segment (%) of N/NSs genes and the M-segment (%) of the polyprotein gene is also worth noting, as these metrics may contribute to the understanding of viral behavior in response to different outbreak scenarios. Overall, this table serves as a crucial resource for researchers aiming to dissect the intricate dynamics of CpG clusters in viral epidemiology, thereby facilitating targeted intervention strategies.
